title: Iranians Voice Shock and Defiance in Face of Trump’s Looming Deadline
summary: President Trump has threatened devastating attacks if Iran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Some Iranians questioned what had happened to American values.

title: Trump Threatens Broad Attacks in Iran. How Is Illegal Conduct in War Defined?
summary: It is illegal for any military to target civilians, as President Trump has suggested he would in threats against Iran. But the U.S. has sought significant leeway in defining a civilian target.

title: Shelly Kittleson, American Journalist Abducted in Iraq, Is Freed
summary: The journalist, Shelly Kittleson, was abducted by a militia allied with Iran and held for a week. Iraqi officials say she was freed in exchange for the release of militia members.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Astronauts Spread ‘Moon Joy’ to the Public
summary: While science can seem colorless and plain, NASA’s lunar crew members have brought expressiveness and emotion about their journey to mission control and the public.

title: ‘Definitely a Sham’: As Tariffs Climb, Trade Fraud and Accounting Tricks Proliferate
summary: U.S. imports from China have shrunk drastically. But billions of dollars of the change appear to be the result of accounting gimmicks and outright fraud.

title: Despite High Oil Prices and Volatile Stocks, Companies See Openings for Deals
summary: Bankers say companies are braving higher oil prices and whipsawing stock prices to seize on the willingness of federal antitrust enforcers to approve mergers.

title: Gulf Backers Invest $24 Billion in Paramount’s Deal for Warner Bros.
summary: Despite speculation that the Iran war could scuttle commitments from Saudi Arabia and other Middle Eastern countries, their investments have been secured.

title: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square Bids to Buy Universal Music Group
summary: The complex transaction pitched by the billionaire hedge fund manager would need to pass muster with the music label’s shareholders.

title: Finasteride for Male Baldness is Rewriting the Rules of Male Beauty
summary: A pill to cure baldness is changing the way men age — and how they see themselves.

title: Texas Considers Required Reading List for Schools, Which Includes the Bible
summary: Education officials are planning an overhaul to English and social studies in the nation’s largest Republican led state.

title: Idaho Cut Services for People With Schizophrenia. Then the Deaths Began.
summary: Eliminating outreach to people with severe mental illness set off such a cascade of bad outcomes that Idaho has scrambled to reverse the cuts.

title: ‘No Labels’ Arizona Wants to Rebrand as the Independent Party
summary: Both the Democratic and Republican parties in Arizona have been locked in a legal battle with a chapter of the group “No Labels” as it tries to rechristen itself.

title: Without Elon Musk, Wisconsin’s Supreme Court Justice Election Goes Quiet
summary: Elections for the Wisconsin Supreme Court have previously brought record-breaking spending and national attention. Tuesday’s race has been a more muted affair.

title: What to Watch in the Georgia Special Election to Replace Marjorie Taylor Greene
summary: Clay Fuller, a Republican allied with President Trump, will face Shawn Harris, a Democrat, in the election to fill the remainder of Ms. Greene’s term after her resignation from Congress.

title: Anthropic Claims Its New A.I. Model, Mythos, Is a Cybersecurity ‘Reckoning’
summary: The company said on Tuesday that it was holding back on releasing the new technology but was working with 40 companies to explore how it could prevent cyberattacks.

title: How Accurate Are Google’s A.I. Overviews?
summary: The company’s A.I.-generated answers look authoritative, but they draw on an array of sources, from trustworthy sites to Facebook posts.

title: What Is Longevity Medicine and How Do You Navigate It?
summary: The field is ripe with opportunity — and opportunism. Here’s how to navigate it.

title: Wireless Festival Canceled After U.K. Blocks Kanye West From Entering Country
summary: Entry to the country is being denied to the rapper formerly known as Kanye West because his presence “would not be conducive to the public good.” The festival he was due to headline is now canceled.

title: Duchamp Made a Urinal Into Art in 1917. We’re Still Discussing It.
summary: Marcel Duchamp changed the face of culture in the 20th century, and beyond, with an unconventional sculpture that challenged how we think of art.

title: A Brief History of 4 Urinals
summary: Marcel Duchamp’s original “Fountain” sculpture vanished within days of its 1917 appearance. He later introduced these versions in response to demand.

title: How Duchamp Inspired These 4 Artists
summary: Decades after his death, he continues to influence contemporary artists. Here are four — of many — who have riffed on his urinal sculpture “Fountain.”

title: How Much Humiliation Can Vance Take?
summary: The vice president gets saddled with the Iran war and eroding popularity.

title: He Was an Insurance Executive. She’s a Doctor. They’re Divided.
summary: We put a doctor and a former insurance executive in a room.

title: The Far Side
summary: The Artemis II crew has gone farther from Earth than anyone has ever been.

title: ICE Agents Shoot Into a Car, Injuring a Suspect in Northern California
summary: A federal official said the agents were pursuing a “wanted gang member” who tried to run one of them over. No confirmation of that account was immediately available.

title: Kim Jong-un’s Daughter Drives a Tank as Talk Accelerates Around North Korea Succession
summary: Images of Kim Ju-ae at the helm of the military vehicle, with her father riding on top, added to speculation that she was being groomed to succeed him as North Korea’s leader.

title: ‘The Late Show With Stephen Colbert’ Being Replaced by ‘Comics Unleashed,’ CBS Says
summary: The comedian and longtime producer will bring his syndicated programming to the late-night slot that “The Late Show With Stephen Colbert” will vacate in May.

title: Protests in Mexico Challenge Move of Frida Kahlo Trove to Spain
summary: Officials scrambled to reassure Mexicans that a collection of esteemed artworks would return by 2028. A rarely-seen will may clarify the collector’s wishes.
